List available built-in scenarios

list_scenarios.Rd

Returns the names that can be passed to load_scenario().

Usage

list_scenarios()

Value

Character vector of scenario identifiers.

Examples

list_scenarios()
#>  [1] "condorcet_cycle_three"                           
#>  [2] "diamond_and_one_extreme"                         
#>  [3] "diamond_and_one_very_extreme"                    
#>  [4] "dougherty_edward_p_u_c_h2012_fig3"               
#>  [5] "dpmr_puch2014_seven"                             
#>  [6] "fiorina_plott_a_p_s_r1978_series1"               
#>  [7] "fiorina_plott_a_p_s_r1978_series1_plott_cond"    
#>  [8] "fiorina_plott_a_p_s_r1978_series3"               
#>  [9] "fiorina_plott_a_p_s_r1978_series3_not_plott_cond"
#> [10] "fiorina_plott_modified"                          
#> [11] "for_plott_seven"                                 
#> [12] "for_uninimity_seven"                             
#> [13] "for_uninimity_three"                             
#> [14] "four_unit_square_corners"                        
#> [15] "laing_olmsted_bear"                              
#> [16] "plott_cond_thirteen"                             
#> [17] "poly_eleven"                                     
#> [18] "poly_nine"                                       
#> [19] "poly_seven"                                      
#> [20] "poly_thirteen"                                   
#> [21] "square_five"                                     
#> [22] "square_four"                                     
#> [23] "square_nine"                                     
#> [24] "symmetrical_eleven"                              
#> [25] "symmetrical_five"                                
#> [26] "symmetrical_five_rotated"                        
#> [27] "symmetrical_nine"                                
#> [28] "symmetrical_nine2"                               
#> [29] "symmetrical_seven"                               
#> [30] "tie_two_alts_four_voters"                        
#> [31] "tovey_regular_polygon"                           
#> [32] "triangle_five"                                   
#> [33] "triangle_three"
